programme.title: The National
programme.desc: CBC's flagship nightly news program, featuring the day's top stories with in-depth and original journalism; Adrienne Arsenault and Ian Hanomansing anchor.

programme.title: CBC News: Morning
programme.desc: The latest news in Canada and around the world.

programme.title: About That
programme.desc: Andrew Chang expands the understanding of the stories everybody's talking about.

programme.title: Today's Top Stories
programme.desc: What's happening across Canada and around the world.

programme.title: About That
programme.desc: Andrew Chang expands the understanding of the stories everybody's talking about.

programme.title: CBC News Special: The King in Canada
programme.desc: Adrienne Arsenault hosts special coverage of the beginning of the King and Queen's historic visit to Canada.

programme.title: CBC News: Morning
programme.desc: What do you need to know about the world around you? Heather Hiscox takes the helm as the anchor for this weekday-morning news program to tell you. Joining her is Colleen Jones with weather and sports info., and Danielle Bochove with all you need to know about the business world.

programme.desc: Andrew Chang expands the understanding of the stories everybody's talking about.

programme.title: CTV News Special: Rick Hansen: Unbreakable 50 Years Later
programme.desc: Rick Hansen returns to the scene of the accident that changed his life forever. CTV’s Sandie Rinaldo talks to the Canadian icon.

programme.title: Question Period
programme.desc: Question Period, the national political landmark program, is a must-see for political junkies.

programme.title: CTV News Special: Rick Hansen: Unbreakable 50 Years Later
programme.desc: Rick Hansen returns to the scene of the accident that changed his life forever. CTV’s Sandie Rinaldo talks to the Canadian icon.

programme.title: W5: Avery Haines Investigates
programme.desc: Avery rides atop Mexico’s “death train” through narco territory, documenting the harrowing journey migrants take in search of a better life.

programme.title: CTV News Special: Rick Hansen: Unbreakable 50 Years Later
programme.desc: Rick Hansen returns to the scene of the accident that changed his life forever. CTV’s Sandie Rinaldo talks to the Canadian icon.

programme.desc: An investigator uses new scientific advances to track a 16-year-old girl's murderer, 50 years later.

programme.title: Country House Hunters New Zealand
programme.desc: Escape the hustle and bustle of city life, with New Zealand home-buyers looking for a lifestyle change.

programme.title: Country House Hunters New Zealand
programme.desc: Matt Gibb meets couple Sue and Ted who are looking for a new home in Kaikoura.

programme.title: Country House Hunters New Zealand
programme.desc: Couple Rainy & Neil have been living on a houseboat for the past 12 months and have fallen in love with the Bay of Islands.

programme.title: Country House Hunters New Zealand
programme.desc: Matt is in the Canterbury countryside with three incredible houses to show Toni.

programme.title: Cross Country Cake Off
programme.desc: The Prairies’ top cake makers meet the judges in Edmonton and four are chosen to bring a “Water World” to life for a spot in the finale.

programme.title: Cross Country Cake Off
programme.desc: Mid-Canada’s cake makers present their edible art. The best move on to bake a “Child’s Play” cake in the Cross Country Cake Off kitchen.

programme.title: Cross Country Cake Off
programme.desc: Mary and Andrew rendezvous in Montreal to taste eight Quebecers’ cakes. The top four bake “After Dark” cakes for a spot in the finale.

programme.title: Cross Country Cake Off
programme.desc: The qualifying round ends in the East with eight Atlantic cake makers. Four are chosen to bake their “Bucket List” cake for the judges.

programme.title: Cross Country Cake Off
programme.desc: The top cake makers from each region come together in the CROSS COUNTRY CAKE OFF kitchen to bake for the grand prize in the national finale.

programme.title: Pressure's On
programme.sub-title: Classic Sunday Dishes Made in Weeknight Time
programme.desc: Get inspired with fast weeknight meals, including Classic Lasagna and Thai Green Curry, with host Vanessa Gianfrancesco.

programme.title: Pressure's On
programme.sub-title: Four Fresh Dinners that are Always on Time
programme.desc: Vanessa prepares teriyaki salmon and quiche Lorraine in a multi-cooker.

programme.title: VegHeadz
programme.desc: Spring into the kitchen with chef Pino Di Cerbo’s fresh seasonal menu.

programme.title: VegHeadz
programme.desc: Chef Pino Di Cerbo serves up brassicas, including stems and stalks from cauliflower and kohlrabi!

programme.title: VegHeadz
programme.desc: Chef Pino Di Cerbo creates a menu featuring sweet and smoky flavours.

programme.title: VegHeadz
programme.desc: Chef Pino Di Cerbo creates dishes featuring melons, including honeydew gazpacho and grilled cantaloupe.

programme.title: VegHeadz
programme.desc: Chef Pino Di Cerbo shares recipes to elevate the humble carrot.

programme.title: VegHeadz
programme.desc: Chef Pino Di Cerbo focuses on the finest of foodie foliage - the leaf!

programme.title: VegHeadz
programme.desc: Chef Pino Di Cerbo creates dishes that celebrate the magic of autumn.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Vegetable lasagna; what to plant in fall; tips on how to throw a zero waste party; stylish DIY storage solutions; best fall travel deals.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Efficient ways to lay out & organize the kitchen, linen closet, & front hallway; cooking expert Mary Berg's make-ahead vegetarian chili.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Clint Howard ("The Dilemma"); relationship solutions from dating guru Andrea Syrtash.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Soap opera icon Eric Braeden; the latest on the Golden Globes.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Actor Christopher Plummer; Taylor Kitsch; chef Mark McEwan.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Suzanne Sumers; fitness guru Tosca Reno.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Actress Wendy Malick; therapist Ashley Howe.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: The Hills' star Whitney Port chats fashion, Bedding 101, and ordering a custom headboard.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Olympian Clara Hughes; super model Chanel Iman.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Len Cariou from Blue Bloods drops by and we get gardening secrets for longer lasting flowers.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Vegetable lasagna; what to plant in fall; tips on how to throw a zero waste party; stylish DIY storage solutions; best fall travel deals.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Efficient ways to lay out & organize the kitchen, linen closet, & front hallway; cooking expert Mary Berg's make-ahead vegetarian chili.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Clint Howard ("The Dilemma"); relationship solutions from dating guru Andrea Syrtash.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Soap opera icon Eric Braeden; the latest on the Golden Globes.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Actor Christopher Plummer; Taylor Kitsch; chef Mark McEwan.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Suzanne Sumers; fitness guru Tosca Reno.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Actress Wendy Malick; therapist Ashley Howe.

programme.title: The Saturn V Story
programme.desc: This is the story of the most powerful machine ever built and a nation who watched on with hope, together.

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: Ride along on dramatic balloon flights into colorful geomagnetic storms to help revolutionize space travel.

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: Cosmologists were stunned to learn the universe is flying apart in all directions at an ever-increasing rate.

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: Did Mars ever develop life? The search for answers reveals the forces that long ago doomed the Red Planet.

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: Examine the Universe's first micro-moments to learn why the Universe is vast almost beyond our comprehension.

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: There are plans to send spacecraft to the Moon to find resources that may one day allow humans to live there.

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: What are scientists learning about the history of the Moon and its relationship to the development of Earth?

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: Massive solar storms attack Earth by disrupting radio signals and navigation systems and harming power grids.

programme.title: Order and Disorder
programme.desc: Jim investigates a strange set of laws that link together everything from engines to humans to stars.

programme.title: Order and Disorder
programme.desc: Jim learns that information isn't just about human communication; it's woven into the very fabric of reality.

programme.title: The Next Great Extinction Event
programme.desc: There have been five major extinction events in our planet's history, are we about to experience the 6th?

programme.title: Atlas of a Changing Earth
programme.desc: This documentary reveals the processes melting glaciers, and explores how rising seas may threaten coastlines.

programme.title: Earth in 1000 years
programme.desc: Earth's stores of ice on Greenland and Antarctica are melting, and sea levels rising. Where is Earth headed?

programme.title: Voyage of Curiosity
programme.desc: Curiosity's robot rover exploring Mars is now known as one of the greatest achievements in space exploration.

programme.title: Shockwave
programme.desc: A powerful earthquake and tsunami will some day devastate the Pacific Northwest. What can we do to prepare?

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.sub-title: Supermassive Black Hole at the Center of the Galaxy
programme.desc: The largest object in our galaxy is a supermassive black hole that dominates its central region.

programme.title: Exploring Venus
programme.desc: The surface of Venus is 95% carbon dioxide with an atmospheric pressure equivalent to 900 meters underwater.

programme.title: Exploring Venus
programme.desc: Surface temperatures reach 450 degrees - any technology we send to Venus wouldn't stand a chance in the heat.

programme.title: Disease Hunters
programme.desc: Scientists are studying genomes of COVID-19 to track the disease's spread and develop lifesaving vaccines.

programme.title: Disease Hunters
programme.desc: The discovery of antibiotics has saved countless lives. But how do we stay one step ahead of "super-bugs"?

programme.title: Disease Hunters
programme.desc: Who can stop the menace caused by deadly mosquito-borne diseases? Mosquitoes kill a million people each year.

programme.title: Voyage of the Continents
programme.sub-title: Oceania: The Tectonic Ring of Fire
programme.desc: Pacific and Australian tectonic plates clash across the South Pacific causing intense volcanic activity

programme.title: China's Long March Into Space
programme.desc: Three men sit atop 500 tons of liquid fuel in China's Shenzhou 7 ship, ready to blast 185 miles into space.

programme.title: Moon Shots
programme.desc: With rare archive footage, this stunning one-hour special brings scientific history to life.

programme.title: Hubble's Enduring Legacy
programme.desc: The Hubble's iconic images are brought to life to answer the most important questions now facing astronomers.

programme.title: Shockwave
programme.desc: A powerful earthquake and tsunami will some day devastate the Pacific Northwest. What can we do to prepare?

programme.title: Zero Gravity: Life on the International Space Station
programme.desc: ESA and NASA astronauts document six months exploring outer space at the International Space Station.

programme.title: Ocean Wild
programme.desc: Off the coast of the Philippines are some of the most amazing sea creatures in the world.

programme.title: Saved From Extinction
programme.sub-title: Horses in Search of a King
programme.desc: A genetic anomaly threatens Przewalski’s wild horses. Can this rare species be saved from extinction?

programme.title: Saved From Extinction
programme.desc: Golden Lion Tamarins narrowly escaped extinction in the 1970s, but will a new intruder spell the end?

programme.title: Saved From Extinction
programme.sub-title: The Turtle Who Came Out of Nowhere
programme.desc: Scientists are approached by a previously undiscovered species of turtle. Does this new animal pose a threat?

programme.title: Saved From Extinction
programme.desc: The oryx has been all but annihilated. Are these rare animals destined for extinction?

programme.title: The Lost Island of Madre de Dios
programme.desc: Watch divers, geologists, and biologists face extreme conditions to study this unexplored Antarctic land.

programme.title: Shark Cinema Science
programme.desc: This underseas journey explores advanced shark research worldwide with acclaimed filmmaker Andy Casagrande.

programme.title: Herders: The Ancient Tradition Lives On
programme.desc: Every year, a master shepherd takes 200 black-headed sheep through the middle of the German capital of Berlin.

programme.title: Herders: The Ancient Tradition Lives On
programme.desc: In Kyrgyzstan, herders drive their herds into the mountains to pastures at nearly 2,500 meters (8,000 feet).

programme.title: Herders: The Ancient Tradition Lives On
programme.desc: In northeastern Uganda, a herder finds that a disease affecting cattle threatens his livelihood.

programme.title: Herders: The Ancient Tradition Lives On
programme.desc: A Raika camel herd has scattered throughout the dry forest of the Rajasthan land in northwest India.

programme.title: Herders: The Ancient Tradition Lives On
programme.sub-title: Living with Alpacas in the Peruvian Andes
programme.desc: The rapidly changing climate threatens the way of life for an alpaca herder in the high Andes of Peru.

programme.title: Africa's Deadly Kingdom
programme.desc: The Kalahari: a thirsty land where brief rains spark vibrant life, soon reclaimed by the desert.

programme.title: Africa's Deadly Kingdom
programme.desc: Africa’s southern coral reefs are vibrant and dynamic worlds in which remarkable creatures that live.

programme.title: Africa's Deadly Kingdom
programme.desc: Africa's vast grasslands host epic wildlife gatherings, where survival depends on adapting to seasons.

programme.title: Africa's Deadly Kingdom
programme.desc: See how the inhabitants of South Africa's beautiful but volatile West Coast continually adapt to survive.

programme.title: Saved From Extinction
programme.sub-title: Horses in Search of a King
programme.desc: A genetic anomaly threatens Przewalski’s wild horses. Can this rare species be saved from extinction?

programme.title: Saved From Extinction
programme.desc: Golden Lion Tamarins narrowly escaped extinction in the 1970s, but will a new intruder spell the end?

programme.title: Saved From Extinction
programme.sub-title: The Turtle Who Came Out of Nowhere
programme.desc: Scientists are approached by a previously undiscovered species of turtle. Does this new animal pose a threat?

programme.title: Saved From Extinction
programme.desc: The oryx has been all but annihilated. Are these rare animals destined for extinction?

programme.title: Wonders of Northern Europe
programme.desc: Finland is a country of geographic and cultural transition; at the same time with a very distinct identity.

programme.title: Wonders of Northern Europe
programme.desc: Iceland is a volcanic island with a magnificent environment. Why did humans decide to colonize Iceland?

programme.title: Wonders of Northern Europe
programme.desc: The Norwegian landscapes still bear traces of this tough geological history.

programme.title: Wonders of Northern Europe
programme.desc: Sápmi becomes coastal along Norway, where it splinters off into thousands of islets.

programme.title: Elephant Refugees
programme.desc: When Botswana became poacher-free, a mass migration of elephants followed. It now is a haven for elephants.

programme.title: Rubber Jellyfish
programme.desc: Balloons' harmful effects, to us and to our environment, have been greenwashed and kept a secret for decades.

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Evolution Earth

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Rewilding: Back to Nature
programme.desc: Worldwide, there are various projects and approaches to give nature more space again. The aim is to create larger wilderness areas that develop with hardly any influence of humans - by reintroducing so-called key animal species and creating a network of wilderness corridors across entire continents. Rewilding promises to be a cost-effective form of conservation in which we can boost biodiversity and protect the climate.

programme.title: Wild Prairie Man
programme.desc: Located in the Northern Great Plains of Canada, Grasslands National Park has been a 20 year odyssey for wildlife photographer James R. Page. For James, critters are more interesting than people and he shares some of his iconic images and experiences from this endangered ecosystem. We showcase the parks wilderness that is dominated by prairie light and dark sky, the wildlife, prairie dogs, mesmerizing landscapes, and bisons. We immerse ourselves in the wild habitat of Grasslands National Park for over a year capturing some never before scenes in the Wild Prairie. James R. Page's connection to the land and the wildlife provides an unforgettable journey into the heart of this endangered ecosystem, while Park experts help us understand its complexity.

programme.title: The Secret of Migrating Birds - Tryst at the Red Sea
programme.desc: Each year in spring, Israel witnesses a unique spectacle. Many millions of migrating birds cross the land bridge between Africa, Asia and Europe.Their first major roosting place after an exhausting nonstop flight over the African deserts is in southern Israel – a vital stopover. Here they replenish their energy reserves. The flocks of migrating birds are pursued by flocks of ornithologists. Hundreds of birders flood into the port city of Eilat on the Red Sea to take part in the Migration Festival. For a week, Israel's southernmost coastal resort is home to international scientists and amateur birders.

programme.title: King of the Mountains - Golden Eagle
programme.desc: Aerial shots of mating Golden eagles, a clash between a Golden and a White-tailed eagle, and an intimate glimpse into their nesting grounds combine to create a spectacular film. Tracking these majestic birds reveals the wild nature of their hunting grounds, showcasing the diverse animal and plant life that share their habitat with the 'king of the skies.' Viewers are brought up close as the eagle effortlessly glides over forests and canyons, abruptly diving in a breathtaking pursuit of prey. Witnessing these birds in their natural habitats is witnessing them at their finest. Embark on a magnificent journey through the skies and landscapes with us.

programme.title: King of the Seas - Sea Eagle
programme.desc: The times where eagles have been seen as varmints are over. They are now perceived as an integral part of the food cycle. Due to the fact that the habitats of the sea eagles in Germany are largely found in the north-eastern regions that are rich in water, the movie accompanies the eagles to an island, home to hundreds of sea ravens, where they rob the baby sea ravens of their food. They seem to always profit from the successes of the fellow creatures in their habitats. This film introduces you to the expert poachers of the animal kingdom.

programme.title: Hummingbirds: Jewelled Messengers
programme.desc: Hummingbirds live at the limits of what is possible. They have the highest metabolism with a heart rate of 1,200 beats per minute of any vertebrate. Their wings beat 50 times a second. They can hover on the spot, and even fly backwards. To our eyes, they are just a blur. But with the aid of high-speed cameras we can slow down their hyperactive lives and look into their secret world.

programme.title: Snowy Owl: Queen of the North
programme.desc: No other owls are capable of surviving the extreme conditions, whereas the snowy owl is able to hunt through six months of bright sunshine and six months of twilight. This film follows a pair of snowy owls as they breed on the tundra of the High Arctic, 300 miles inside the Arctic Circle. Lemming numbers are low this year. Five owlets hatch and they face a struggle if any are going to survive.

programme.title: Missing Butterflies
programme.desc: Its miraculous transformation from caterpillar to remarkable winged beauty has been a point of constant intrigue. However, in the last few years, many have observed a decrease in the butterfly population. A much-discussed topic in the entomological society is the decline of the insect populace, but to what extent is this topic relevant to our beloved butterflies? For years, a teacher from lower Bavaria and a biologist from Munich have dedicated themselves to studying the butterfly and their presence in Bavaria. The results of their studies tell an enlightening story that is sure to entertain.

programme.title: Unnatural Selection
programme.desc: The wings of cliff swallows in Nebraska are getting shorter – while female turtles in Chesapeake Bay are getting larger. On the Galapagos Islands, two species of finches are developing back into one. Increasing numbers of peregrine falcons are living in urban areas – and have adapted their behaviour: thanks to the city lights, they have learned to hunt at night.

programme.title: Leap Of The Lynx
programme.desc: On the Iberian Peninsula, primeval landscapes are practically non-existent, so the Iberian lynx has lived on cultivated land for centuries – cattle, horse and pig pastures, cork oak forests, timberland, and even holy sites of pilgrimage. The diversity and beauty of these locations are truly stunning and their character and history as relevant to this story as that of the big cat itself. Until five years ago, both populations of Iberian lynx were quickly fading away. Their fragmented habitats were shrinking even further, they were starved by lack of prey and reduced by epidemics. But now, local authorities, scientists, landowners, and other groups have finally begun to act in unison.

programme.title: Lions On The Move
programme.desc: Kevin Richardson shares an extraordinary bond with Africa’s most fearsome predators. Together with them, he has experienced some real adventures. However, transporting 27 lions and 14 hyenas to a new home presents a different challenge.The lions' new home will be a sanctuary on the edge of Dinokeng, a South African game reserve spanning 18,000 square metres. The huge task ahead includes planning and designing the new enclosures, and securing permits to move the animals.

programme.title: Lions On The Move
programme.desc: Not only are the animals regularly rotated into new enclosures to prevent repetitive behaviour developing out of boredom, but Kevin also has to practice loading them in and out of the van. Can the reluctant lions and hyenas eventually be persuaded to get on board with the move?

programme.title: Elephants: Back to the Wild
programme.sub-title: Elephants: Back to the Wild: The Orphanage of Elephants - Nairobi Nationalpark
programme.desc: We follow the rescue of a baby elephant. Once rescued, it will be transported by plane to the breeding station and raised with love and commitment. In order to satisfy the little pachyderm’s urge to move while in the breeding station, there is sometimes an adorable football match between the animals.

programme.title: Elephants: Back to the Wild
programme.sub-title: Elephants: Back to the Wild: The Return of the Elephants - Tsavo-East-Nationalpark
programme.desc: We accompany the first steps of a two-year-old elephant who is just starting his release program. We also observe the story of two young elephants in their first night in the wilderness on their own. Captivating film footage allows us to truly learn how elephants behave in the wild.

programme.title: Rewilding: Back to Nature
programme.desc: Worldwide, there are various projects and approaches to give nature more space again. The aim is to create larger wilderness areas that develop with hardly any influence of humans - by reintroducing so-called key animal species and creating a network of wilderness corridors across entire continents. Rewilding promises to be a cost-effective form of conservation in which we can boost biodiversity and protect the climate.

programme.title: Wild Prairie Man
programme.desc: Located in the Northern Great Plains of Canada, Grasslands National Park has been a 20 year odyssey for wildlife photographer James R. Page. For James, critters are more interesting than people and he shares some of his iconic images and experiences from this endangered ecosystem. We showcase the parks wilderness that is dominated by prairie light and dark sky, the wildlife, prairie dogs, mesmerizing landscapes, and bisons. We immerse ourselves in the wild habitat of Grasslands National Park for over a year capturing some never before scenes in the Wild Prairie. James R. Page's connection to the land and the wildlife provides an unforgettable journey into the heart of this endangered ecosystem, while Park experts help us understand its complexity.

programme.title: The Secret of Migrating Birds - Tryst at the Red Sea
programme.desc: Each year in spring, Israel witnesses a unique spectacle. Many millions of migrating birds cross the land bridge between Africa, Asia and Europe.Their first major roosting place after an exhausting nonstop flight over the African deserts is in southern Israel – a vital stopover. Here they replenish their energy reserves. The flocks of migrating birds are pursued by flocks of ornithologists. Hundreds of birders flood into the port city of Eilat on the Red Sea to take part in the Migration Festival. For a week, Israel's southernmost coastal resort is home to international scientists and amateur birders.

programme.title: King of the Mountains - Golden Eagle
programme.desc: Aerial shots of mating Golden eagles, a clash between a Golden and a White-tailed eagle, and an intimate glimpse into their nesting grounds combine to create a spectacular film. Tracking these majestic birds reveals the wild nature of their hunting grounds, showcasing the diverse animal and plant life that share their habitat with the 'king of the skies.' Viewers are brought up close as the eagle effortlessly glides over forests and canyons, abruptly diving in a breathtaking pursuit of prey. Witnessing these birds in their natural habitats is witnessing them at their finest. Embark on a magnificent journey through the skies and landscapes with us.

programme.title: King of the Seas - Sea Eagle
programme.desc: The times where eagles have been seen as varmints are over. They are now perceived as an integral part of the food cycle. Due to the fact that the habitats of the sea eagles in Germany are largely found in the north-eastern regions that are rich in water, the movie accompanies the eagles to an island, home to hundreds of sea ravens, where they rob the baby sea ravens of their food. They seem to always profit from the successes of the fellow creatures in their habitats. This film introduces you to the expert poachers of the animal kingdom.

programme.title: Hummingbirds: Jewelled Messengers
programme.desc: Hummingbirds live at the limits of what is possible. They have the highest metabolism with a heart rate of 1,200 beats per minute of any vertebrate. Their wings beat 50 times a second. They can hover on the spot, and even fly backwards. To our eyes, they are just a blur. But with the aid of high-speed cameras we can slow down their hyperactive lives and look into their secret world.

programme.title: Snowy Owl: Queen of the North
programme.desc: No other owls are capable of surviving the extreme conditions, whereas the snowy owl is able to hunt through six months of bright sunshine and six months of twilight. This film follows a pair of snowy owls as they breed on the tundra of the High Arctic, 300 miles inside the Arctic Circle. Lemming numbers are low this year. Five owlets hatch and they face a struggle if any are going to survive.

programme.title: The marvellous World of French Rivers
programme.desc: The Dordogne consists of a visible and an invisible system of flowing water. Caves and basins under the earth's surface store the excess water and return it to the river when it is dry. A perfect management of a precious resource, not invented by humans, but by the complex nature itself. The result is an ever life-giving river.

programme.title: Chiemgau, A Changing Landscape
programme.desc: A few thousand years ago, there were bare moraine ridges and barren meltwater valleys here. A landscape born in the last ice age, characterized by the edge of the Alps, gentle hills, moors, and lakes. And by cultural richness, not only on the islands in Lake Chiemsee, with their famous monasteries and castles. Long before King Ludwig II, the first people settled in the Chiemgau region. Finds of hunting weapons from the Neanderthal era prove that the land was inhabited even during the ice age, probably because there was plenty of game to hunt. During the Bronze Age, one of the most important trade routes from the mountains to the Alpine foothills passed through here. Then came the Celts and the Romans.

programme.title: Chiemgau, A Changing Landscape
programme.desc: Three times in the past two decades, the Chiemsee area has experienced "once-in-a-century" floods. Climate, wildlife, and landscape have changed, and it seems this transformation is accelerating. Species living in the flood zones are developing sophisticated survival strategies. Knot ants, for instance, place their queen in the middle and form a raft with their bodies, even conquering new habitats. Flooding is less of a disaster for nature and impacts humans more significantly.

programme.title: Wild Faces of Switzerland - Winter in the Swiss Alps
programme.desc: In the heart of Europe lies Switzerland. It’s a surprisingly big little country: small in area, but big on natural beauty. And, like its many languages and cultures, its scenic diversity is something really special, too: from mighty Alps, dense forests and lush green valleys, to mysterious moors and roaring waterfalls. Mediterranean lakes and craggy rock landscapes are all close together in one small geographical location. Switzerland has many wild faces – some familiar and some hidden. It’s a country that’s astonishingly rich in variety, wilderness and scenic wonder. Each episode of the series seeks to do justice to this diversity.

programme.title: Wild Italy: Top Predators
programme.desc: Peregrine falcons and Herring gull have settled in the center of Rome, now nesting in large numbers.

programme.title: Sperm Whales: Dealing with the Unexpected
programme.desc: The Sperm Whale family in the Crozet Islands faces threats from humans, fishing lines, and deep-sea monsters.

programme.title: Trees Of Life
programme.desc: See how humble plants triggered Earth’s greatest transformation

programme.title: Addos Elephants
programme.desc: Protected elephants live and thrive in Addo Elephant National Park.

programme.title: Lions On The Move
programme.desc: Kevin Richardson, animal lover, faces the challenge of relocating 28 lions and 14 hyenas.

programme.title: Searching For The Coast Wolves
programme.desc: Shot in the rain forests of Canada's west coast, this documentary records the life of the rare coastal wolves.

programme.title: Storks - A Village Rooftop Saga
programme.desc: Ruhstadt, Germany, hosts Germany's largest white stork colony, despite facing damage to roads, roofs

programme.title: Masters Of The Wild
programme.desc: Animals adapt to hostile landscapes using specialized bodies, diets, and behaviors, such as scorpions

programme.title: Animal Armoury
programme.desc: Bears, owls, and tigers use sharp claws for hunting, while baboons, wolves, and woodchucks use their teeth

programme.title: Animal Armoury
programme.desc: Take a look at various animals fighting styles such as elks, gorillas, Kangaroos and shrimp.

programme.title: Animal Armoury
programme.desc: Animals like the Arctic fox, green tree frog, and penguin benefit from coloration

programme.title: White Wolf
programme.desc: Filmed in Yellowstone National Park, this film follows the packs of two rare alpha female White Wolves.

programme.title: White Wolf
programme.desc: Filmed in Yellowstone National Park, this film follows the packs of two rare alpha female White Wolves.

programme.title: Face To Face With A Polar Bear
programme.desc: Jerome Bouvier's film follows polar bear family's journey in Spitzberg, highlighting their education & traits.

programme.title: Adopted By Dolphins - Flipper's Family Secrets
programme.desc: Researchers are accepted as companions by a population of bottlenose dolphins in the Red Sea

programme.title: Sperm Whales: Dealing with the Unexpected
programme.desc: The Sperm Whale family in the Crozet Islands faces threats from humans, fishing lines, and deep-sea monsters.

programme.title: Of Shark And Man
programme.desc: One man’s attempt to tell an untold story finds him in the middle of a shark feeding frenzy.

programme.title: Blue Whisper
programme.desc: Blue Whisper is a 3D-documentary film that explores underwater communication among fish

programme.title: Killer Whales - Up Close and Personal
programme.desc: Delphine, a young killer whale, navigates the Crozet Islands, learning to hunt penguins and sea creatures.

programme.title: Be A Predator
programme.desc: When the world freezes solid, only the fiercest predators endure. See who survives.

programme.title: Savage Wild
programme.desc: Manny is severely bitten by a large western diamond back rattlesnake in south Texas.

programme.title: Animal Armoury
programme.desc: Bears, owls, and tigers use sharp claws for hunting, while baboons, wolves, and woodchucks use their teeth

programme.title: Animal Armoury
programme.desc: Take a look at various animals fighting styles such as elks, gorillas, Kangaroos and shrimp.

programme.desc: Animals like the Arctic fox, green tree frog, and penguin benefit from coloration

programme.desc: Filmed in Yellowstone National Park, this film follows the packs of two rare alpha female White Wolves.
